posts.name: Craig Teets
posts.cooked:
Hi Everybody,

I just saw the reviews from Bourghound (Allen Meadows) on Guffens-Heynen 2022’s. His reviews were shockingly bad. I’ve never seen Allen write anything like before. Here’s some examples.

Wine Example #1
“I can only describe as having a dish soap character”

Wine Example #2
“Once again the nose exhibits a strong dish soap character
along with prominent phenolic elements where the soapy quality can also be found on the palate before terminating in a bitter, unpleasant and abruptly short finale”

Wine Example #3
“Skunky reduction overshadows the fruit”

What’s your opinion on this? Maybe we can hear from the honorable William Kelley.

posts.name: Benjamin Leth Andersen
posts.cooked:
Any info on where he tried the wines?

If you, as a wine reviewer, start to pick-up dish soap notes in multiple wines, it would probably be prudent to retry in another setting before pulishing reviews - unless they were tasted at the domaine

In any case, lets hope this deter just a few potential buyers =) =)

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: That certainly hasn’t been my experience with the 2022s… From-bottle reviews coming out soon.

posts.cooked: Thanks William. It’s nice to hear your opinion.

posts.name: Dennis Atick
posts.cooked: Bourghound.? Never heard of him. I wonder what Burghound reviews are like.

posts.name: Wooju Lee
posts.cooked:
I tried the 22 Macon 1er Jus a week ago. I don’t remember the 21, 20 being anything close to what I tasted on release.

It was quite disjointed, and felt quite oaky (shocking) to the point it was reminiscent of Cali chard. I can certainly see what might be meant by dish soap.

But the nice stuff is all there. It just felt like it is going to need a bit of time for it to settle down. We decanted it and it improved over the evening, although never seemed to completely settle.

FWIW, the person who brought it drank the 22 Juliette the day prior and said this experience was similar, and it was much better the next day.

Probably going to be bottles I am just gonna toss to storage and forget about

posts.name: Benjamin Leth Andersen
posts.cooked: Can you share that bit?

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked: “I would add that Guffens uses a reductive style along with the Diam 30 brand of composite cork so if you’re tempted to open any of these young, be sure to decant them first. Given the less than complimentary scores and comments, I would observe that I have historically been a big supporter of the Guffens wines; indeed they often receive the highest marks of any domaine in the Mâconnais. Such is not the case however in 2022; in fact, I’m not sure what happened but I did not find them to be at their usual level of quality.”

posts.name: Glen Gold
posts.cooked: Thanks for posting that - I’m still confused about the “soap” note, though. I have a 6-pack of mixed 2022 Guffens and wasn’t planning on cracking them anytime soon - very curious to know what WK says when he reviews them in bottle.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
Whilst I haven’t ever tasted the Guffens wines, 2022 was a pretty weird Maconnais vintage, of the softest and least interesting (globally speaking) of any in recent memory. A few producers had some strong points (Guillot Broux and Barraud come to.mind), but…
I tasted the wines of some 20 or so estates, and there were few that had the kind of tension that is my preference.
I look forward to trying the 2023s next month, but am already fearing a similar profile.

posts.name: G av_1n 40r es1
posts.cooked:
Don’t have a horse in the race regarding these specific wines, but appreciate that Allen is willing to write his honest opinion. I found his introductory remarks to be very fair and responsible framing.

I think it’s unlikely that he didn’t control for glassware or swap when he encountered that note and more likely these aren’t to his taste or there was variation. We’ll never know.

posts.name: Mike Kelly
posts.cooked: Had the Macon 1er Jus last evening and it was, to me, excellent - outclassing a couple “heavy hitters.” No soap!
